questions.question: Who were the 'Samantas'?
questions.correct_answer: Big landlords or warrior chiefs acknowledged by existing kings
questions.explanation: They were expected to bring gifts for their kings, be present in their courts, and provide them with military support.

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Maha-samanta'?
questions.correct_answer: A title taken by samantas when they gained power and wealth
questions.explanation: It signifies 'the great lord of a region'. Sometimes they declared their independence from their overlords.

questions.question: What was the 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual?
questions.correct_answer: A ritual meaning 'the golden womb' performed to become a Kshatriya
questions.explanation: It was thought to lead to the 'rebirth' of the sacrificer as a Kshatriya, even if he was not one by birth.

questions.question: What are 'Prashastis'?
questions.correct_answer: Inscriptions that contain details about the achievements of rulers
questions.explanation: They were often composed by learned Brahmanas and might not be literally true, but show how rulers wanted to depict themselves.

questions.question: What was the 'Tripartite Struggle'?
questions.correct_answer: A long-drawn conflict between the Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala dynasties
questions.explanation: The struggle was for control over the city of Kanauj in the Ganga valley.

questions.question: Who was Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.correct_answer: A ruler from Afghanistan who raided the subcontinent 17 times
questions.explanation: His targets were wealthy temples, including that of Somnath in Gujarat, to gather wealth for his capital.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Chahamana (Chauhan) ruler?
questions.explanation: He defeated the Afghan ruler Sultan Muhammad Ghori in 1191, but lost to him the very next year in 1192.

questions.question: Which Chola ruler is considered the most powerful?
questions.explanation: He became king in 985 and expanded Chola control over most of the areas in South India.

questions.question: What were the settlements of peasants called under the Cholas?
questions.explanation: These settlements became prosperous with the spread of irrigation agriculture.

questions.question: What were the associations of traders called?
questions.explanation: These associations also occasionally performed administrative functions in towns.

notes.title: Chola Bronzes
notes.content: Chola bronze images are considered amongst the finest in the world; most were of deities, but some were of devotees.

notes.title: Land Grants
notes.content: Kings often rewarded Brahmanas by grants of land, which were recorded on copper plates given to those who received the land.

notes.title: Sabha
notes.content: An assembly of prominent Brahmana landholders that looked after Brahmadeya (land gifted to Brahmanas).

facts.fact: Dantidurga, a Rashtrakuta chief, overthrew his Chalukya overlord in the mid-eighth century.
Kalhana wrote a long Sanskrit poem containing the history of kings who ruled over Kashmir.
Vetti was a tax taken not in cash but in the form of forced labour.
